Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

SpTU 5, 312 [sale of rab-banutu prebend][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
oo NaN  (start of obverse missing)
o 1'o 1'

[...] ⸢meri⸣-[ba A-šu₂ ša₂ mDU—A A mŠU—dna-na-a it-ti mdUTU—NUMUN—GIŠ]

(o 1') [...]. Eriba, [son of Mukin-apli, descendant of Gimil-Nanaya], agreed a price [together with Šamaš-zer-lišir, son of Šamaš]-iddin, descendant of Rab-bani [...] ... for 2 minas [2 shekels ...] and then, (Eriba) paid the price [...] ....

o 2'2'

[A-šu₂ ša₂ mdUTU]—⸢MU⸣ A lu₂GAL-⸢DU₃⸣ [...] x ⸢ki⸣ 2 ⸢MA⸣.[NA 2 GIN₂]

o 3'3'

[KU₃.BABBAR ša₂ gin₃-nu GANBA] ⸢im⸣-be₂-e-ma ŠAM₂ [...] x GANBA U₄ 18-⸢KAM₂⸣ [...]

(o 3') On the 18th [...], Šamaš-zer-lišir, son of Šamaš-iddin, descendant of Rab-bani, went before Eriba. He spoke to Eriba, saying in the following way:

o 4'4'

[md]⸢UTU⸣—NUMUN—GIŠ A-šu₂ ša₂ mdUTU—MU A lu₂GAL-DU₃ a-na pa-ni meri-ba ⸢DU⸣

o 5'5'

⸢a-na⸣ meri-ba iq-bi ⸢um⸣-ma ŠA₃ a-na 2 MA.NA 2 GIN₂ <KU₃.BABBAR> ša₂ gin₃-nu x [...] AK

(o 5') "The price of my prebend you gave me for the 2 minas 2 shekels of silver with the ginnu-mark ... [...] ... that arable land of 3 pānu 3 sūtu is (too) small."

o 6'6'

ŠE.NUMUN a₄ 3(BARIG) 3(BAN₂) ŠAM₂ GIŠ.ŠUB.BA-ia₂ ta-⸢ad⸣-din-ni i-ṣi

o 7'7'

⸢ak-ki⸣ 1/2 MA.NA 4 GIN₂ KU₃.BABBAR ša₂ gin₃-nu e-lat 2 MA.NA 2 GIN₂ KU₃.BABBAR

(o 7') As 1/2 mina 4 shekels of silver with the ginnu-mark in addition to the 2 minas 2 shekels that Eriba gave to Šamaš-zer-lišir were in deficit.

o 8'8'

m⸢eri⸣-ba a-na <m>dUTU—NUMUN—GIŠ id-din LUGUD₂⸢ri⸣-ma 1/2 MA.NA

(o 8') Šamaš-zer-lišir, son of Šamaš-iddin, descendant of Rab-bani took [...] ... 1/2 minas [4 shekels of silver] with the ginnu-mark, the price of his prebend for his full payment, in the hands of Eriba, son of Mukin-apli, descendant of Gimil-Nanaya.

o 9'9'

[4 GIN₂ KU₃.BABBAR] ša₂ gin₃-nu mdUTU—NUMUN—GIŠ A-šu₂ ša₂ mdUTU—MU A lu₂GAL-DU₃ ŠAM₂

o 10'10'

<<ŠAM₂>> GIŠ.ŠUB.BA-šu₂ ki-ma ⸢ka?-sap?⸣ TILtu₂ ina ŠU-MIN meri-⸢ba⸣ <<DIŠ>>

Bottom
b.e. 1b.e. 1

A-šu₂ ša₂ mDU—A A mŠU—dna-na-a iṣ-[bat ... 3(BARIG)] 3(BAN₂) ŠE.NUMUN ZA₃.<LU> ŠE.⸢BAR⸣

(b.e. 1) [... 3 pānu] 3 sūtu of arable land, tenency dues in barley, beside the city wall and the Harriṣu-canal and ...+1 cubits upper width [beside] the Harriṣu-canal.

b.e. 22

DA BAD₃ u₃ ḫa-ri-iṣ u₃ x x 1 KUŠ₃ SAG.KI AN.TAtu₄

b.e. 33

[DA ḫa]-⸢ri-iṣ⸣ x 41 KUŠ₃ SAG.KI KI.TAtu₄ [DA] A.ŠA₃ ŠA₃ A.ŠA₃

(b.e. 3) ... 41 cubits lower width beside the field belonging to the field of [...].

Reverse
r 1r 1

[x x (x)] 41 KUŠ₃ US₂ ANu₂ DA A.ŠA₃ ša₂ mDIN A <ša₂> mšu-la-⸢a⸣ u₃ lu₂GAL-DU₃tu₂

(r 1) 41 cubits upper length beside the field of Balaṭu, son of Šulaya, descendant of Rab-bani and rab-banûtu-prebend of ... [...].

r 22

ša₂ x [...] x KUŠ₃ US₂ KI.TAu₂ DA A.ŠA₃ ŠA₃ A.ŠA₃ x [...] x x [...]

(r 2) ... cubits lower length beside the field belonging to the field of ... [...] ... [...] estate with date palms of any 3 days of rab-banûtu-prebend.

r 33

x x [...]-⸢MEŠ⸣-šu₂ E₂ gišGIŠIMMAR ša₂ 3-TA u₄-me lu₂GAL-DU₃u₂-[tu₂]

r 44

x ⸢DA⸣ [...] x ⸢GANBA?⸣ mdUTU—NUMUN—GIŠ im-⸢mi⸣-[id]-ma

(r 4) ... [...] ... the price Šamaš-zer-lišir imposed and so he provided (the field) to Eriba, [son of] Mukin-apli, descendant of Gimil-Nanaya.

r 55

a-na m⸢eri⸣-[ba A-šu₂ ša₂] ⸢m⸣DU—A A mŠU—dna-na-a u₂-ša₂-[aṣ]-bi-it

r 66

lu₂mu-kin-nu ⸢md60⸣—DIN-su—E A-šu₂ ša₂ mdUTU—MU—DU A mki-din—dAMAR.UTU

(r 6) Witnesses: Anu-balassu-iqbi, son of Šamaš-šum-ukin, descendant of Kidin-Marduk; Nabu-ah-iddin, son of Hahhuru; Arad-Šamaš, son of [...]-ah-iddin, descendant of Marduk-napšu; Iqiša, son of Mukin-apli [...] ...; Libluṭ, son of Bel-ah-ušabši [...]; Nanaya-iddin, son of Nidinti-Anu, descendant of Rab-bani; [...]

r 77

⸢mdNA₃⸣—ŠEŠ—MU A-šu₂ ša₂ mḫa-aḫ-ḫu-ru mARAD—dUTU A-šu₂ ša₂

r 88

[... x]—ŠEŠ—MU A mdŠU₂—nap-šu₂ mBAša₂ A-šu₂ ša₂ mDU—A [...]

r 99

r 1010
